action.reasoning: Observation: Current best LPIPS 0.6021 (slight gain from 0.6101) but many errors on specific objects/views (e.g. 1f0b4a... with extreme elev~88deg): 'TriplaneNeRFRenderer.forward() missing 1 required positional argument: 'rays_d''. Custom get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth (rel_az, robust eps=1e-8 up-vector) + white BG input works on val but renderer call in predict.py is wrong (causing fallback to default orbit). Analysis: Root cause is incorrect renderer invocation (order/args for rays_o/rays_d/triplane from scene_codes); default orbit mismatches dataset target poses (varying az ~100-300deg relative). Correct call + output-to-RGBA handling will enable exact per-target pose matching for major LPIPS drop. TripoSR zero-shot remains optimal (task match, <1B params, no train). Decision: Update inspect_render.py (self-contained with get func + signatures + 4 likely renderer call variants tried on val sample with json/cameras) to identify exact working API/output format (e.g. renderer(rays_o, rays_d, scene_codes) or kwarg order), then we can port to predict.py. Run inspect to diagnose before full test/submit.
